Package org.eventb.core.seqprover.tactics.tests

Class TreeShape

java.lang.Object
org.eventb.core.seqprover.tactics.tests.TreeShape
public abstract class TreeShape extends Object
Common implementation for verifying rule applications to a proof subtree. The intent is to simplify writing tests about tactic application on a proof node (or more exactly the subtree rooted at that node).

Clients should use code similar to: 

 assertRulesApplied(node, conjI(empty, empty, empty));
where node is a proof tree node and the second parameter is the expected shape of the proof tree rooted at the given node.

      public static void assertRulesApplied(IProofTreeNode node, TreeShape expected)
      Ensures that the proof subtree rooted at the given node as the shape described by the expected parameter.
      Parameters:
      node - a proof tree node
      expected - a description of the expected proof subtree shape
